payload.title: Exhibit Book Finance Committee Meeting 7/17/2023
payload.document_title: Metropolitan Transportation Authority Interim Financial Statements as of and for the Three-Month Period Ended March 31, 2023
payload.description: Consolidated interim financial statements, management's discussion and analysis, notes, and supplementary information for MTA as of March 31, 2023 and for the three-month periods ended March 31, 2023 and 2022. Prepared for Finance Committee meeting on July 17, 2023.

warnings: source.document_title is accepted as passthrough, but prefer title. Document title label.

source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Established in 1965 under Section 1263 of the New York Public Authorities Law, the MTA is a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York whose mission is to continue, develop and improve public transportation and to develop and implement a unified public transportation policy in the New York metropolitan area.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA" or "MTA Group") was established under the New York Public Authorities Law and is a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York
The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") was established in 1965, under Section 1263 of the New York Public Authorities Law

payload.description: Provides subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit Authority ("MTA New York City Transit") and its subsidiary, Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority ("MaBSTOA"), provide subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City.

payload.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between New York City and Long Island

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Long Island Rail Road Company ("MTA Long Island Rail Road") provides passenger transportation between New York City ("NYC") and Long Island.

payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in New York State and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company ("MTA Metro-North Railroad") provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities

payload.description: Operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority ("MTA Bridges and Tunnels") operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City.

payload.description: Operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bus Company ("MTA Bus") operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York.

payload.raw_value_text: down 68.5 million trips (-19.9%) below 2020 first quarter ridership

evidence_refs.source_quote: Preliminary MTA system-wide ridership for first quarter 2023 continued to fall short of the pre-pandemic level, with ridership down 68.5 million trips (-19.9%) below 2020 first quarter ridership.

payload.description: Grand Central Madison terminal was opened for LIRR service in January 2023. Full LIRR service to Grand Central Madison began in February 2023, resulting in a 41% increase in overall LIRR service.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In January of 2023 the Grand Central Madison terminal was opened for LIRR service.
Full LIRR service to Grand Central Madison began in February

tool_args.label: FHWA issues FONSI for CBD Tolling Program

payload.event_name: Finding of No Significant Impact for Central Business District Tolling Program
payload.description: FHWA issued a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) for the Central Business District Tolling Program Environmental Assessment. Contractors have up to 310 days from this approval to complete the design, development, testing and installation of the tolling system.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This process concluded in late June 2023, when the FHWA issued a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI)

payload.event_name: New York State fiscal year 2023-2024 budget enacted
payload.description: Budget includes adjusting Payroll Mobility Tax for largest businesses within NYC to 0.6% generating approximately $1.1 billion; $300 million in one-time State aid; requiring NYC to contribute $165 million for paratransit services; $65 million to reduce fare increase; expanding service frequencies on subway and launching a pilot program providing five free bus routes in NYC.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On April 27, 2023, the New York State fiscal year 2023-2024 budget was enacted which includes critical investments to the MTA.

payload.description: Capital programs totaling $55,442 in capital expenditures covering the years 2020-2024 for commuter railroad operations and transit system. Originally approved by MTA Board on September 25, 2019. Amended by MTA Board on July 27, 2022. Revised 2020-2024 MTA Bridges and Tunnels Capital Program totaling $3,327.
